notes:
**Joint release with keen-backend 0.22.0** — Secure federated invites, smarter managed-server updates, and navigation polish.

## Federated sharing (invite secure delivery)

- **Single-use claim links** — invite URLs now carry a one-time claim secret instead of a standing access token. The bearer credential is minted only when someone accepts the invite.
- **Recipient landing page** — opening an invite link in a browser shows project name and access level before you join (no metadata leaks without the secret).
- **Accept from the app** — paste an invite link in Settings or open a `keen://invite` deep link; Keen confirms what you're joining, then connects.
- **Email an invite** — from the Share dialog, email an invite directly when Google is connected and your server has a public HTTPS URL. Resend pending invites from the management list.
- **Address book** — save contacts and groups in Connections; share with a whole group in one action.
- **Change access without re-inviting** — adjust a subscriber's permission tier in place (no revoke + re-mint).

## Managed hosting (Keen Cloud)

- **Update your server** — clearer copy throughout ("Update your server" instead of operator jargon). Staged progress while the server restarts, version verification when it comes back, and an authoritative "Up to date" state so you don't roll twice onto the same image.
- **Fewer false alarms** — the "Backend Unreachable" modal stays hidden during an intentional server update.

## Navigation & polish

- **Favorites everywhere** — your pinned shortcuts now appear in Settings, Chat, and detail-page sidebars, not just the main list rails.
- **Inactive servers tucked away** — destroyed/failed managed servers collapse behind a toggle instead of cluttering Settings.
- **Dev stability** — `npm run dev` no longer breaks when Rust doc artifacts exist under `target/doc`.

## Install

| Platform | Artifact |
|---|---|
| Apple Silicon | `Keen_0.22.0_aarch64.dmg` |
| Intel Mac | `Keen_0.22.0_x64.dmg` |

Existing installs: **Keen → Check for Updates** or Settings → Updates.

Managed Keen Cloud users: after updating the desktop app, use **Update your server** in Settings → Managed Servers (or the compatibility prompt) once the new backend image is available.
